data_model: Uses oauth_accounts and sessions from infra-001; sessions.elevated_until timestamp added.

description: Create organization flow with plan picker, setup form, slug validation, contact email, ownership type, and terms acceptance.
ui_details: Signed-in page with GitHub-style plan picker cards for Free, Team, and Enterprise. MVP highlights Free with direct Create organization CTA and treats paid plans as disabled/info-only. Setup form is centered and compact with title 'Tell us about your organization', organization name input, live URL preview, contact email input, radio buttons for personal account vs business/institution, conditional company name input, terms checkbox, inline validation callouts, and green Next/Create button.
behavior: Clicking Create a free organization opens the setup form. Typing a name normalizes spaces to hyphens in the preview and validates availability/reserved words without submitting. Submit creates the organization only after required fields and terms are valid, then redirects to /{org} or /organizations/{org}/settings/profile. Reserved names, duplicates, invalid email, and rate limits render inline errors. No billing checkout or paid-plan provisioning in MVP.
data_model: Reads users and reserved_slugs. Writes organizations (slug, display_name, public/contact email, terms_of_service_type, company_name), organization_memberships with creator as owner, organization_policy_settings defaults, organization_audit_events, and optional organization_social_accounts later.

description: Render and save organization profile/settings shell with public profile fields, contact fields, context switcher, and danger-zone affordances.
ui_details: Organization settings shell with top organization nav, left grouped settings sidebar, context switcher, and right content cards. General form includes organization display name, public email, description, URL, four social account inputs, searchable location selector, billing/contact email fields, Save buttons per section, rename organization control, archive organization dialog, and delete organization danger dialog requiring typed slug.
behavior: Owners can update public profile fields independently and see success/error flash messages. Context switcher navigates between personal and organization settings. Rename validates slug availability and requires confirmation. Archive/delete controls open confirm-name dialogs and are disabled when destructive prerequisites are not met. Unsupported billing/payment pages are omitted or disabled because billing is out of scope.
data_model: Reads/writes organizations, organization_social_accounts, organization_memberships, organization_policy_settings, organization_audit_events, organization_verified_domains, user permissions, and S3 organization avatars when avatar upload is implemented.

description: Manage organization people, invitations, membership visibility, owner/member roles, exports, and removal confirmations.
ui_details: Organization People page under org nav with tabs for Members, Outside collaborators, Pending collaborators, Invitations, Failed invitations, and Security Managers. Main toolbar has member search, disabled bulk action until rows selected, Export dropdown with JSON/CSV, and green Invite member button. Member rows show checkbox, avatar, display name, username, 2FA badge, public/private membership visibility menu, role label, teams count, roles count, row action menu, and membership source.
behavior: Search filters members. Invite member opens a dialog with username/email autocomplete, role/team choices, and Send invitation. Invitations send SES email, expire after 7 days, and can be retried/canceled from pending/failed tabs. Visibility menu toggles public/private membership. Role change opens confirmation. Removing or demoting the final owner is blocked with a structured error. Exports download current filtered members as JSON or CSV.
data_model: Reads/writes organization_memberships, organization_invitations, teams, team_memberships, users, user_email_addresses, organization_audit_events, notification/email delivery records, and permission-derived 2FA/session status flags. Exports read the filtered member query.

description: Build organization teams list, empty state, team creation form, nested-team rules, visibility, notifications, mentions, and team permissions foundation.
ui_details: Teams page uses organization shell. Empty state has three explanatory columns for flexible repository access, request-to-join teams, and team mentions, plus New team and Learn more CTAs. New team form has Team name, Description, Parent team selector or empty 'no teams' text, visibility radio group with Visible recommended and Secret, team notification radio group Enabled/Disabled, and green Create team button.
behavior: Team list filters teams when present and links to team detail. Creating a team slugifies the name, validates uniqueness, and blocks secret nested teams. Visible teams can be discovered and @mentioned by organization members; secret teams are visible only to members and cannot be nested. Enabled notifications notify members on team mentions. Team permissions cascade from parent to child for repository authorization and review requests.
data_model: Reads/writes teams, team_memberships, team_repository_permissions, organization_memberships, repositories, repository_permissions, notification_subscriptions, organization_audit_events, and team mention indexes. Parent-child constraints prevent cycles and secret-team nesting.

description: Implement organization member privilege and repository policy settings that control base permissions, repository creation, forking, discussions, projects, Pages, app access, and team creation.
ui_details: Long organization settings page with independent cards/forms. Base permissions card uses action menu options None, Read, Write, Admin plus confirmation. Repository creation card has public/private checkboxes and Save. Additional cards include private repository forking checkbox, repository discussions checkbox, Projects base permission action menu, Pages public/private publishing checkboxes, app access request radio controls, repository visibility/delete/transfer toggles, issue deletion toggle, and team creation toggle. Each card uses compact explanatory copy and per-card Save buttons.
behavior: Only organization owners can change settings. Base permission changes require confirmation and immediately affect repository authorization for members while preserving explicit higher permissions. Policy-locked controls render disabled with a why link. Repository creation settings constrain /new owner/visibility choices and Rust create endpoints. Forking, discussions, Pages, app access, delete/transfer, issue deletion, and team creation settings are enforced by Rust API paths and logged to org audit events.
data_model: Reads/writes organization_policy_settings, organizations default permission fields, repository_permissions, organization_memberships, repositories, pages_sites, teams, organization_audit_events, and security_audit_events. Policy writes include actor, old/new JSON values, and optional enforced_by metadata.

description: Render repository commit history with branch/tag, author, and date filters plus grouped commit rows.
ui_details: Repository shell with Commits heading and compact toolbar: branch selector button, All users author selector, and All time datepicker. Branch selector dialog has searchable Find a branch input, Branches/Tags tabs, selected default badge, branch radio rows, and View all branches link. Commit list groups rows by date; each row has linked subject, linked PR number, optional expand-message button, author avatar/login, relative time, status-check summary button, Verified/Partially verified badge, short SHA button, browse-at-commit icon, and row overflow menu.
behavior: Loading the page resolves the selected ref and streams commits grouped by commit date. Selecting a branch/tag reloads commit history for that ref. Selecting an author filters to that author and preserves branch/date state. Datepicker selection applies until/before date filtering; Clear returns to all time. Clicking short SHA or subject opens commit detail. Clicking status opens checks summary; clicking browse opens the repository tree at that commit.
data_model: Reads repositories, repository_permissions, repository_git_refs, commits, commit_signature_verifications, commit_status_summaries, users, pull_requests, check_runs, and branch_activity_snapshots. Writes recent_visits and optional per-user commit-list filter telemetry only.

description: Render commit detail with verification, parent links, checks, file tree, searchable diff, and copy/browse controls.
ui_details: Repository shell with Commit heading, short SHA, Browse files button, copy/full SHA control, author avatar/login, relative authored time, status-check summary, Verified badge, subject/body, branch link, linked PR link, parent commit link, and diff summary. Diff area has collapsible file tree, draggable pane splitter, Filter files input, Search within code input, file rows with changed-line counts, per-file more menu, Expand all lines buttons, and side-by-side diff grid with old/new line numbers.
behavior: Opening a commit validates repository visibility and SHA existence. Browse files navigates to the repository tree at that commit. Copy SHA copies the full hash. Parent link opens parent commit. File tree selection scrolls/focuses the matching diff file. Search within code highlights matches inside visible diff content. Expand controls fetch extra context lines. Binary or large diffs show bounded placeholders with Raw/View file actions.
data_model: Reads repositories, repository_permissions, commits, commit_file_changes, commit_signature_verifications, commit_status_summaries, check_runs, pull_requests, repository_git_refs, git_trees, git_objects, users, and code_search_index path records. Writes recent_visits and optional diff display preferences.

description: Render repository branches page with Overview/Active/Stale/All tabs, branch search, branch metadata, protection badges, and row actions.
ui_details: Repository shell with Branches heading, tablist for Overview, Active, Stale, and All, plus Search branches input. Overview page separates Default and Active branches in dense tables. Columns are Branch, Updated, Check status, Behind, Ahead, Pull request, and Action menu. Rows include branch link, copy branch-name button, protected shield/link, latest author avatar, updated time, status-check count, ahead/behind numbers, linked draft/open PR number, and kebab menu with Activity and View rules.
behavior: Tabs change the branch classification list without losing repository context. Search performs case-insensitive substring filtering over branch names. Clicking a branch opens the code tree for that ref. Copy copies branch name. Protected badge opens branch rules/protection. PR number opens linked pull request. Activity opens branch activity, View rules opens applicable rulesets/branch protection. Destructive delete/restore actions stay permission-gated and are hidden or disabled for read-only public inspection.
data_model: Reads repositories, repository_permissions, repository_git_refs, branch_activity_snapshots, commits, commit_status_summaries, pull_requests, branch_protection_rules, repository_rulesets, users, teams, and check_runs. Writes recent_visits and optional branch search telemetry; branch delete/restore/rename later write refs, audit events, and notifications.

description: Render repository Pulse activity summary with period selector, overview metrics, top committers chart, and recent release/PR/issue activity.
ui_details: Repository Insights shell with left sidebar links for Pulse, Contributors, Community standards, Commits, Code frequency, Dependency graph, Network, Forks, Actions usage metrics, and Actions performance metrics. Main Pulse pane shows exact date range, Period button, overview cards for active PRs/issues, linked counts for merged/open PRs and closed/new issues, summary sentence with authors/commits/files/additions/deletions, accessible Top Committers bar chart, avatar links, and compact activity lists for releases and merged pull requests.
behavior: Opening Pulse computes aggregates for the selected period. Period menu supports 24 hours, 3 days, 1 week, and 1 month, updates URL/query state, and reloads metrics. Metric links navigate to filtered issue or pull request lists. Top Committers chart exposes a data-table fallback. Activity item links open releases, pull requests, users, or commit ranges.
data_model: Reads repositories, repository_permissions, commits, commit_file_changes, users, pull_requests, issues, releases, repository_activity_events, and repository_insight_snapshots. Writes cached repository_insight_snapshots and optional recent_insight_views; no user content writes.

description: Render repository contributor analytics with repository-wide commit chart, period controls, range sliders, and top contributor commit charts.
ui_details: Insights shell with Contributors selected. Header states default branch scope and commit-limit message when line counts are omitted. Controls include Period button, chart action/export buttons, View as data table button, and Highcharts-style accessible region. Main chart is Commits over time with start/end range sliders. Contributor sections show avatar, login, total commits link, chart menu, and individual bar chart.
behavior: Page loads top contributors for the default branch and excludes merge/empty commits. Period selector and range sliders constrain visible weekly data. Chart data-table button opens tabular values. Contributor login opens profile; commit count opens commit history filtered to that author. Repositories over the commit threshold omit addition/deletion line counts and show explanatory text.
data_model: Reads repositories, repository_permissions, repository_git_refs, commits, commit_authors, users, repository_contributors_weekly, commit_signature_verifications, and repository_insight_snapshots. Writes cached contributor weekly rollups and chart cache rows.

description: Render repository traffic analytics for push-access users, including clones, visitors, referring sites, and popular content.
ui_details: Insights shell with Traffic selected. Permissioned content shows two compact line charts for full clones and visitors over the past 14 days, chart hover/tooltips, summary totals/unique counts, referrers table ordered by views/unique visitors, popular content table with path/title links, and a UTC/timezone note. Users without push access see a repository-safe redirect or 403 empty state without leaking traffic counts.
behavior: Traffic route requires push access. Clones and visitor charts update hourly; referrers and popular content update daily. Hovering or keyboard-focusing a chart point reveals exact date/count/unique values. Referrer links navigate externally with safe rel attributes; content links open repository paths. API returns structured 403 when permission is missing.
data_model: Reads repositories, repository_permissions, repository_traffic_daily, repository_referrers_daily, repository_popular_content_daily, users, and audit/access logs. Writes daily/hourly traffic rollups from request/git clone events and optional chart cache rows.

description: Render repository network graph and forks list with period/type/sort filters and fork metadata.
ui_details: Network page shows Insights sidebar, Network graph heading, explanatory copy about recent commits to the repository network, daily update note, and links into fork/tree views. Forks page has Switch to tree view link, Period menu, Repository type menu, Sort menu, Defaults Saved/Save defaults button, and dense fork rows. Rows show fork owner avatar/name, repository name, metric icons for stars/forks/issues/PRs where available, Created relative time, and Updated relative time.
behavior: Network graph loads the 50 most recently pushed forks and refreshes from daily projections. Fork filters update URL state and list content; defaults can be saved when filter state differs. Period supports multiple windows, repository type filters active/inactive/archived/starred-like classifications, and sort supports Most starred plus recency/order options. Fork rows open owner or fork repository links without mutating upstream data.
data_model: Reads repositories, forks, repository_network_forks, repository_git_refs, commits, users, stars, issues, pull_requests, repository_permissions, and saved_fork_filter_defaults. Writes network/fork projection cache rows and optional per-user saved_fork_filter_defaults.

description: Render dependency graph dependencies, dependents, package filtering, ecosystem filtering, and SBOM export.
ui_details: Dependency graph page uses Insights shell with Dependencies/Dependents tabs and Export SBOM button. Dependencies tab has Search all dependencies query-builder input, total count, Ecosystem select-panel filter, and dependency rows showing package name/version, Direct/Transitive badge, row options menu, ecosystem, manifest/lockfile path, detected date, and license. Dependents tab has Package filter menu, repository/package count summary, warning disclosure, owner filter menu with username input, and dependent repository rows with owner/name and metrics.
behavior: Dependency extraction indexes supported manifest/lockfile changes. Search filters dependencies by package text and ecosystem. Row menu opens package details or advisory views when available. Export SBOM starts a server job and downloads a signed artifact. Dependents package filter switches package scope; owner filter narrows dependent repositories. Dependency graph disabled/unavailable states return structured 422 and render a clear empty state.
data_model: Reads repositories, repository_permissions, dependency_manifests, repository_dependencies, dependency_packages, repository_dependents, dependency_advisories, sbom_exports, users, stars, forks, and packages. Writes dependency extraction results, dependents index rows, SBOM export jobs/artifacts, and audit events for export.

description: Render repository security overview and SECURITY.md policy viewing/editing.
ui_details: Repository workspace with active Security and quality tab and a left sidebar headed Security and quality. Sidebar groups include Overview, Findings, Dependabot, Code scanning, Secret scanning, Reporting, Security policy, and Advisories. Main overview shows rendered SECURITY.md content, recent advisory rows with severity/status pills, and feature cards for Dependabot/code scanning/secret scanning. Policy page renders Markdown with heading anchors, email links, file action menu, and maintainer Start setup/Edit policy CTA.
behavior: Opening /security shows public policy and published advisories for readers while hiding private alert counts. Security policy setup creates or edits SECURITY.md through the normal repository file editor and commit/propose-change flow. Missing policy shows an empty state with Start setup for maintainers and a read-only missing-policy message for readers. Editing policy updates the real Git tree so Code/blob/raw views reflect the file.
data_model: Reads repositories, repository_permissions, repository_security_feature_settings, repository_security_policies, git_objects, commits, repository_security_advisories. Writes repository_security_policies, git_objects, commits, repository_git_refs, repository_activity_events, security_audit_events when maintainers create or edit policy files.

description: Render and manage Dependabot vulnerability alerts from dependency manifests.
ui_details: Security and quality shell with Dependabot selected. Header has Dependabot alerts title, Give feedback link, alert-settings kebab, Open/Closed tabs, search bar, filter dropdowns for package/ecosystem/manifest/scope/severity, sort menu defaulting to Most important, selectable alert rows, and disabled empty state with settings link. Alert rows show package ecosystem icon, package name, advisory title, severity pill, Development/Production label, manifest path, fixed version, state, and checkbox.
behavior: When disabled, show the observed disabled callout and link to repository vulnerability settings. Enabled lists support search/filter/sort, row selection, bulk dismiss/reopen, and direct links to alert detail pages. Detail pages show vulnerable dependency, advisory, affected/fixed versions, timeline, assignee selector, security update PR CTA when supported, dismiss dropdown with reason/comment, and reopen for dismissed alerts. Dismiss/reopen writes audit events and notification updates.
data_model: Reads repository_security_feature_settings, dependency_manifests, repository_dependencies, dependency_packages, dependency_advisories, dependabot_alerts, users, teams, pull_requests, repository_permissions. Writes dependabot_alerts state, security_alert_events, pull_requests for generated security updates, notifications, security_audit_events.

description: Render and manage code scanning alerts from SARIF uploads and Actions analysis.
ui_details: Security and quality shell with Code scanning selected. Empty state says Code scanning is not enabled with Enable code scanning link. Enabled state has search box, Filters dropdowns for state/severity/tool/branch/ref/tag/application-code, open/closed counts, alert rows with rule title, severity/security-severity pill, tool name, file path and line, branch/default-branch badge, linked issue chip, and updated time. Detail view shows code snippet, path breadcrumbs, alert message, Show paths disclosure, Show more remediation guidance, assignee selector, dismiss controls, and linked issue actions.
behavior: SARIF uploads from Actions or REST create code scanning runs and normalize alerts by rule/location/fingerprint. Opening list defaults to open alerts on the default branch. Search supports free text and bounded qualifiers; invalid qualifiers show inline errors. Readers can see PR annotations, but repository write/admin permission is required for the summary list, dismissal, assignment, and link-to-issue actions. Disabled state does not leak historical alert counts.
data_model: Reads repository_security_feature_settings, code_scanning_runs, code_scanning_alerts, workflow_runs, check_runs, issues, repository_git_refs, commits, users, repository_permissions. Writes code_scanning_runs, code_scanning_alerts, security_alert_events, issue links, notifications, security_audit_events, S3 SARIF upload records.

description: Render and manage secret scanning alerts and push-protection outcomes.
ui_details: Security and quality shell with Secret scanning selected. Disabled state has Secret scanning alerts heading, explanatory text about accidental sensitive information, and Enable secret scanning link. Enabled list has provider/default and generic results tabs, query input, filters for provider/secret type/validity/resolution/bypassed/state/team/topic/sort, alert rows with redacted secret type, provider badge, path/line, validity pill, assignee, bypassed marker, and created/updated time. Detail page shows redacted evidence, file location, commit, validity, resolution form, assignee selector, and timeline.
behavior: Secret scanning indexes pushed commits and existing blobs when enabled. Push protection blocks or warns on matching secrets during Rust Git push; bypasses require reason and create alert events. Users can filter alerts, assign owners, resolve as revoked/false positive/used in tests/won't fix, reopen unresolved alerts, and inspect timeline events. Plaintext secrets are never displayed, logged, or stored; only fingerprints and redacted snippets are shown.
data_model: Reads repository_security_feature_settings, secret_scanning_alerts, secret_scanning_patterns, git_objects, commits, users, teams, repository_permissions. Writes secret_scanning_alerts, security_alert_events, security_audit_events, notifications, push_protection_bypasses, and redacted evidence metadata.

description: Create, publish, and view repository security advisories with package, CVE, CVSS, CWE, credits, and collaboration metadata.
ui_details: Security and quality shell with Advisories selected. List rows show status icon, title, GHSA id, draft/published state, relative publish/update date, author avatar/login, severity pill, and pagination. Detail page shows large title, severity badge, author/state, GHSA id, package ecosystem/name, affected versions, patched versions, Markdown description/details, CVSS score button/modal, CVSS base metrics table, CVE id, CWE disclosure, credits, and maintainer edit/publish controls.
behavior: Readers can view published advisories. Maintainers can create draft advisories with required title plus optional CVE, package ecosystem/name, affected/patched versions, severity/CVSS, CWE, credits, and Markdown details. Drafts stay private to authorized collaborators, can add advisory collaborators/credits, can publish to public list, and can be edited with audit events. Pagination and severity filters keep URL state; invalid CVSS/CWE/package fields show inline validation.
data_model: Reads repository_security_advisories, repository_security_advisory_credits, users, teams, repositories, packages, security_audit_events, repository_permissions. Writes repository_security_advisories, advisory credits/collaborators, private fork references where implemented, notifications, security_audit_events, and optional dependency advisory feed rows.

description: Render repository Discussions list with pinned discussions, category rail, search, labels, state filters, sorting, and upvotes.
ui_details: Repository workspace with active Discussions tab. Main column shows heading, Pinned Discussions cards with author avatars/category chips, query-builder search input defaulting to `is:open`, Sort by details menu, Label select-panel, Filter action menu for Open/Closed/Answered/Unanswered/Locked/Pinned, green New discussion button, and dense discussion rows. Right rail shows category links with emoji/name, Most helpful contributor list for Last 30 days, and community links such as Code of conduct and project website.
behavior: Typing a query updates URL-backed `discussions_q` state and filters rows. Category links append category qualifiers and change the page title/category header. Sort options reorder rows by latest activity, newest, top, most commented, and similar menu choices. Label/status filters compose into the search query. Upvote buttons increment/decrement the current user's vote, update counts optimistically, and require authentication. Empty categories show a category-specific empty state with New discussion CTA.
data_model: Reads repositories, repository_permissions, discussions, discussion_categories, discussion_labels, labels, discussion_comments, discussion_votes, users, repository_community_links, and discussion_pins. Writes discussion_votes, saved discussion query state where implemented, and activity events for vote changes.

description: Create discussions through category chooser, category-specific forms, Markdown composer, required similar-search acknowledgement, and poll creation.
ui_details: Category chooser shows selectable cards for each category with emoji, name, description, Answers enabled badge when applicable, and Get started links. Create form shows selected category summary, Choose a different category link, title input, category-form fields rendered from `.github/DISCUSSION_TEMPLATE/*.yml`, Markdown write/preview tabs with full toolbar, attachment dropzone/file input, required `I have done a search for similar discussions` checkbox, Start discussion button, helpful resources sidebar, and first-time community reminder callout. Poll categories render question/options controls instead of normal answer-enabled form fields.
behavior: New discussion opens category selection first unless category is preselected. Selecting a category loads the matching YAML discussion form and validates required fields before submit. Preview renders Markdown without creating the discussion. Similar-search link runs a query from the typed title and the checkbox is required on observed forms. Submit creates a discussion, initial body/comment/form answers, optional poll/options, notification subscriptions, and redirects to the detail page. Invalid YAML falls back to a generic title/body composer; polls cannot use category forms.
data_model: Reads repositories, repository_permissions, discussion_categories, discussion_category_forms from Git files, users, saved_replies, and repository_community_links. Writes discussions, discussion_form_answers, discussion_comments for the body, discussion_polls, discussion_poll_options, subscriptions, notifications, attachments/S3 objects, and repository_activity_events.

description: Render discussion detail pages with timeline comments, replies, reactions, answer marking, sidebar metadata, subscription controls, and close/answered states.
ui_details: Detail page shows title, discussion number, Open/Closed/Unanswered or Answered status, answered-by summary and jump-to-answer link when present, category breadcrumb, author avatar/login, collaborator badges, permalink/edit history controls, Markdown-rendered body, upvote button, reaction menu, comments/replies timeline, Oldest/Newest/Top sort links, nested replies, write-a-reply composer, and right sidebar for Category, Labels, Participants, Notifications, and Events. Answered discussions show a highlighted Answered by card and View full answer link.
behavior: Readers can sort comments, expand replies, copy permalinks, subscribe/unsubscribe, upvote, and react when authenticated. Comment composer supports write/preview, saved replies, attachments, and validation. Users with triage or greater access can mark/unmark a comment as answer in answer-enabled categories, close/reopen with reasons, edit category/labels, and moderate comments. Events timeline records close, reopen, answer-marked, category changes, pins, locks, transfers, and deletions.
data_model: Reads discussions, discussion_comments, discussion_comment_replies, discussion_categories, labels, discussion_labels, discussion_answers, discussion_votes, reactions, users, subscriptions, notifications, discussion_events, repository_permissions. Writes discussion_comments/replies, discussion_answers, discussion state, discussion_events, subscriptions, notifications, reactions, discussion_votes, and attachments/S3 objects.

description: Manage discussion categories, sections, category formats, emoji/name/description metadata, and discussion category form templates.
ui_details: Manage categories page has a list of category rows and section headers, New category and New section buttons, pencil/trash row actions, emoji picker, name input, description textarea, format selector for Announcement/Open-ended/Poll/Question and Answer, optional section assignment, and confirmation dialogs. Discussion category forms are edited through repository file editor with `.github/DISCUSSION_TEMPLATE/FORM-NAME.yml`, syntax-highlighted YAML, previewable form schema, commit/propose-change controls, and validation errors.
behavior: Write/admin users can create up to 25 categories with unique emoji/name pairs, edit details and format, nest categories in one section, create/edit/delete sections, and delete categories by choosing a destination category for moved discussions. Category format changes affect creation/detail behavior but do not silently corrupt existing discussions. YAML form commits on default branch are parsed into create forms; malformed schemas show maintainer-visible errors and reader-safe fallback forms. Poll categories cannot use discussion category forms.
data_model: Reads repositories, repository_permissions, discussion_categories, discussion_category_sections, discussions, git_objects, repository_git_refs, commits, users. Writes discussion_categories, discussion_category_sections, discussion category migrations, git_objects/commits for form templates, repository_activity_events, and audit events.

description: Moderate and manage discussions through pinning, locking, transferring, deleting, recategorizing, closing, and converting issues to discussions.
ui_details: Moderator controls appear in the discussion sidebar and action menus: Pin discussion, Pin to category, Edit pinned discussion, Unpin, Lock conversation with Allow reactions checkbox, Transfer discussion repository picker, Delete discussion confirmation, Category gear menu, Close discussion dropdown with reason, and issue sidebar Convert to discussion dialog. Pinned discussion customization modal supports title/body preview and pin target selection. Destructive dialogs use explicit warnings and disabled/confirm states.
behavior: Users with triage/write/admin permissions can recategorize non-poll discussions, pin up to four global and four category-specific discussions, lock/unlock with optional reactions, close/reopen resolved/duplicate/outdated discussions, transfer only within same owner constraints, and delete discussions after confirmation. Issue conversion copies issue title/body/comments metadata into a new discussion category and links the source issue event. All moderation actions create audit/events rows, update notifications, and enforce permissions server-side.
data_model: Reads discussions, discussion_categories, discussion_pins, discussion_locks, discussion_events, issues, issue_comments, repositories, organizations, repository_permissions, users. Writes discussion state/category/pin/lock rows, discussion_events, converted discussion records from issues, issue timeline events, notifications, audit events, and optional deletion tombstones.

description: Render and operate discussion polls with poll category list/detail, voting controls, results, comments, and poll-specific constraints.
ui_details: Polls category page mirrors the Discussions list but title/header show Polls, category qualifier chip, poll emoji on every row, Polls category description, and poll discussion rows with upvote/comment counts. Poll creation form uses poll question, option inputs, add/remove option controls, optional Markdown body, preview tab, attachment controls where supported, and Start discussion button. Poll detail shows poll option rows with radio/check controls, vote button or inline vote action, percentage/count result bars after voting, comments/replies timeline, reactions, and sidebar metadata.
behavior: Poll category filters list to `category:Polls`. Creating a poll requires at least two non-empty options and prevents discussion category forms. Authenticated users can cast one vote and may change it only if the chosen poll policy allows; anonymous users see results-disabled or sign-in prompt state. Results update after vote, remain permission-aware for private repositories, and preserve comments/reactions like normal discussions. Poll discussions cannot be moved to or from non-poll categories.
data_model: Reads discussions, discussion_categories, discussion_polls, discussion_poll_options, discussion_poll_votes, discussion_comments, users, repository_permissions, reactions, subscriptions. Writes discussion_polls/options/votes, discussions, discussion_comments, notifications, activity events, reactions, and audit events for moderator poll changes.

description: Implement user, organization, and repository Projects v2 list pages with search, open/closed tabs, templates tab, sort menu, status badges, and copy-project flow.
ui_details: Organization Projects page uses the org profile shell with active Projects tab and count badge, a dismissible Welcome to Projects banner, Projects/Templates sub-tabs, search input labeled Search all projects with `is:open` query, Open and Closed count tabs, Sort action menu, and dense project rows. Rows show project title, optional description, project number, updated timestamp, optional status pill such as On track, and More project options menu. Copy dialog includes project title prefilled with `[COPY]`, Include draft issues checkbox, Cancel, and Copy project submit button.
behavior: Typing in search filters by title/description/status and updates query state. Open/Closed tabs switch list scope. Sort supports recent update/name/created-like options and persists in the URL. Clicking a project row opens the project workspace. Copy opens a modal, optionally includes draft issues, creates a new project with copied views/fields/workflows where allowed, and shows permission errors for viewers without write access. Repository Projects tab lists projects linked by default repository or explicit repository link.
data_model: Reads projects, project_repositories, project_status_updates_latest, project_permissions, organizations, users, repositories, project_templates, project_counts, and viewer roles. Writes copied projects, project_views, project_fields, project_workflows, project_items when copying is authorized, plus audit events and recent_visits.

description: Implement the Projects v2 table workspace with saved view tabs, filter bar, field columns, grouping/slicing, editable cells, item add row, and unsaved-view state.
ui_details: Project workspace has a compact global header breadcrumb, project title, View/Insights/Settings controls, horizontal saved-view tabs, plus-button for new view, side slice rail, filter query bar with tokenized qualifiers, matching item count, View configuration button, and virtualized high-density table. Table rows show issue/PR/draft icons, repository/name/number, title, labels, status, assignees, iteration/date/single-select/text/number fields, grouped headers with counts, draggable row numbers, field header menus, and bottom omnibar add row.
behavior: Changing filters, sorting, field order, grouping, slicing, or hidden fields marks the view as unsaved until saved. Clicking a cell opens an inline editor appropriate to the field type. Dragging rows reorders manual order; dragging grouped rows updates their grouped field value where permitted. Bottom add row accepts pasted issue/PR URLs, `#` repository search, bulk add, draft issue creation, and issue creation modal. Field value clicks add filter tokens. Permissioned edits sync back to linked issues/PRs and create timeline events.
data_model: Reads projects, project_views, project_fields, project_items, project_item_field_values, issues, pull_requests, draft_issues, repositories, labels, milestones, users, project_permissions, and project_saved_sorts. Writes project_items, project_item_field_values, project_view_state, draft_issues, issue/PR metadata when fields map to native metadata, item timeline events, audit events, and notifications.

description: Implement Projects v2 board and roadmap layouts with layout switching, columns, swimlanes, limits, roadmap date fields, markers, and zoom.
ui_details: View menu shows Layout choices Table, Board, and Roadmap with keyboard hints `t`, `b`, and `r`, plus Fields, Column by, Swimlanes, Sort by, Field sum, and Slice by menu rows. Board layout renders horizontally scrollable columns from a single-select or iteration field, column headers with item counts and optional limits, cards with title/repo/labels/assignees/field chips, Add item affordances, and empty column toggles. Roadmap layout renders grouped rows beside a timeline with start/target/iteration bars, date-field selector, marker selector, Month/Quarter/Year zoom, and draggable horizontal/vertical panes.
behavior: Switching layout changes the active view layout and saves for authorized users. Dragging a card between board columns updates the column field value. Column limit warnings highlight columns over limit but do not block additions. Board swimlanes group cards horizontally by field. Roadmap uses selected start and target date or iteration fields, supports markers for iterations/milestones/item dates, zoom changes timeline density, and preserves filter/slice/sort state across layout changes.
data_model: Reads project_views, project_fields, project_items, project_item_field_values, project_board_column_settings, project_roadmap_settings, issues, pull_requests, draft_issues, milestones, iterations, and project_permissions. Writes project_views layout/settings, project_item_field_values from drag/drop, column limits, roadmap date/marker/zoom settings, and audit events.

description: Implement Projects v2 custom fields and iterations, including field creation, single-select options, date/text/number values, iteration cycles, breaks, and field deletion/rename controls.
ui_details: Project settings field page has a left settings sidebar, fields list with built-in and custom fields, New field button, field detail pane, name input, type selector, option rows with color swatches for single-select, date/text/number help text, iteration settings with Starts on date picker, duration stepper and days/weeks selector, Add iteration button, More options for custom iteration dates, Insert break affordance, Save changes, Rename, and Delete confirmation.
behavior: Users with write/admin project access can create up to the field limit, rename fields, reorder/select options, add/delete single-select values, create iteration fields with three default iterations, add future iterations, edit iteration names/date ranges, insert breaks, and delete fields after confirmation. Deleting a field removes field values from project items but not linked issues/PRs. Iteration filters support `@current`, `@previous`, `@next`, comparison operators, and ranges.
data_model: Reads projects, project_fields, project_field_options, project_iterations, project_iteration_breaks, project_item_field_values, and project_permissions. Writes project_fields, project_field_options, project_iterations, project_iteration_breaks, field rename/delete audit events, and invalidates project view/filter caches.

description: Implement project items, draft issues, linked issue/PR sync, conversion of draft issues to issues, archive/restore, and item side panel editing.
ui_details: Project item side panel opens from a row/card and shows title, item type icon, repository/source link, body/description editor for drafts, assignees, labels, milestone, status/custom field editors, comments/activity area, Convert to issue button for drafts, Archive/Remove actions, and close button. Draft issue rows use a draft icon and project-only metadata. Archive page shows archived item list with filters and restore controls.
behavior: Add item supports issue/PR URL paste, repository search using `#`, bulk add from repository filters, issue creation modal, and draft issue creation. Draft issues can be edited without notifying mentioned users, assigned within project fields, converted to repository issues by choosing repository/labels/milestone, and then gain a normal issue URL. Archiving removes items from active views while preserving history; restore returns them. Linked issue/PR metadata changes in project sync to the source issue/PR and vice versa.
data_model: Reads project_items, draft_issues, issues, pull_requests, repositories, project_fields, project_item_field_values, users, labels, milestones, comments, archive state, and project_permissions. Writes project_items, draft_issues, project_item_field_values, converted issues, issue timeline events, project_item_events, archived/restored state, notifications for converted issues, and audit events.

description: Implement built-in project workflows and automation for status updates, add-item rules, auto-archive, close-on-status, merged/closed Done transitions, and Actions/GraphQL automation hooks.
ui_details: Workflows page uses the project settings shell with Default workflows list, workflow cards with enabled/disabled toggles, event description, Edit button, condition/filter builder, target field/value selectors, repository selector for auto-add, archive criteria form, Save and turn on workflow button, and activity/status messages. Disabled workflows show Turn on CTA and explanatory empty state. Automation attribution is displayed as `@github-project-automation` equivalent.
behavior: Default workflows initialize with closed issue/PR -> Done and merged PR -> Done enabled. Authorized users can enable/disable workflows, edit event conditions, set status values when items are added/closed/merged/reopened, auto-add issues/PRs from selected repositories that match filters, auto-archive matching completed items, and optionally invoke project automation from Actions or GraphQL mutations. Workflow executions update project field values, add timeline events, and never bypass repository/project permissions.
data_model: Reads project_workflows, project_workflow_rules, project_fields, project_field_options, repositories, issues, pull_requests, project_items, project_permissions, and actions_workflow_runs. Writes project_workflows, project_workflow_rules, project_item_field_values, project_item_events, workflow_execution_logs, audit events, and notifications where linked issue/PR state changes.

description: Implement project settings, permissions, repository linking, templates, visibility, status updates, README/description, close/delete, and organization-level project policy.
ui_details: Settings page has left navigation for General, Access, Fields, Workflows, Templates, and Danger Zone. General form includes project title, short description, README Markdown editor, visibility dropdown, default repository selector, status update panel with state selector On track/At risk/Off track/Complete, start date, target date, Markdown message, and Save buttons. Access page lists collaborators/teams with role dropdowns. Templates page has Set as template toggle and copy-source information. Danger Zone has Close project and Delete project confirmations.
behavior: Admins can rename projects, change visibility where org policy permits, set default repository so new issues route there and repository Projects tab links back, add/remove repository links, manage user/team access, publish status updates, set project as template, close/reopen, and delete after confirmation. Organization policy can disable projects, restrict visibility changes, and set base project permissions. All access and danger actions require server permission checks and audit events.
data_model: Reads projects, project_readmes, project_status_updates, project_access_grants, teams, users, project_repositories, organization_policy_settings, project_templates, and project_permissions. Writes project metadata, readme revisions, status updates, visibility/default repository/repository links, access grants, template flags, closed/deleted state, and organization/project audit events.

description: Implement Projects v2 Insights charts with default burn-up chart, custom charts, filters, ranges, chart data table, and project status/charts sharing.
ui_details: Insights page switches from project view to an Insights header with Return to project view link, selected Insights nav item, left sidebar sections for Default charts and Custom charts, default Burn up chart link, chart title/description, filter bar, matching item count, range links for 2 weeks/1 month/3 months/Max, Custom range button, chart action buttons, accessible Highcharts-style region, and View as data table button. Custom chart form supports chart title, filters, chart type, X/Y/grouping fields, and visibility to project viewers.
behavior: Opening Insights preserves project context and renders charts from project items. Filter changes recompute matching item count and chart series. Range controls update burn-up window; custom range opens date selection. View as data table toggles accessible tabular chart data. Authorized users can create/edit/delete custom charts and share them with viewers; readers can view chart data but not mutate charts. Status updates surface in project lists and side panels.
data_model: Reads projects, project_items, project_item_field_values, project_fields, project_charts, project_chart_series_cache, project_status_updates, issues, pull_requests, draft_issues, and project_permissions. Writes project_charts, chart configuration revisions, chart cache snapshots, status updates, and audit events for chart mutations.

description: Render repository wiki home and page-reading experience with Markdown content, page sidebar, custom sidebar/footer, and clone URL.
ui_details: Repository workspace shell with Wiki tab selected. Main column shows wiki page title, rendered Markdown body with heading anchors, revision link near the title, and permissioned Edit/New Page buttons when available. Right column shows page list/contents module with chevron expanders for per-page table of contents, Show more pages button, custom sidebar rendered from `_Sidebar.*`, custom footer rendered from `_Footer.*`, and `Clone this wiki locally` input containing `https://host/{owner}/{repo}.wiki.git` plus copy button.
behavior: Opening `/wiki` redirects to or renders the Home page. Clicking a page link navigates to `/wiki/{slug}` and refreshes title/body/sidebar active state. Expanding a page chevron lazy-loads that page's table of contents. Show more pages expands the truncated page list. Copy clone URL writes the wiki Git remote URL to the clipboard. Missing/disabled/private wikis show an explicit disabled or not-found state based on repository visibility and viewer permission.
data_model: Reads repositories.has_wiki, repository_permissions, wiki_repositories, wiki_pages, wiki_page_revisions, rendered_markdown_cache, wiki_sidebar_pages, wiki_footer_pages, and users for author metadata. Writes clipboard-only UI state; page rendering writes no database rows except optional rendered Markdown cache refreshes.

description: Implement wiki page list, create, edit, preview, save, and local Git-backed publishing workflow.
ui_details: Pages index has heading `Pages`, optional New Page button for editors, and a bordered list of all wiki pages. Each row uses a document icon, page-title link, and `Last updated` relative timestamp. Create/edit pages use a title input, Edit mode dropdown for Markdown or supported markup extensions, Markdown/code editor with toolbar including image insert, Write/Preview tabs, rendered preview pane, Edit message commit-message field, Save Page button, and validation/error flash area.
behavior: Pages index lists all pages sorted by title with updated timestamps and links to detail pages. New Page opens an empty editor; Edit opens the selected page content. Save validates page title characters, extension/edit mode, permissions, and non-empty commit message, then commits to the wiki Git repository default branch and redirects to the rendered page. Preview renders Markdown/markup without saving. Image insert prompts for image URL and alt text. Local Git pushes to `{repo}.wiki.git` update live pages only when pushed to the wiki default branch.
data_model: Reads wiki_pages, wiki_page_revisions, wiki_repositories, repository_permissions, users, supported_markup_formats, and rendered_markdown_cache. Writes wiki_pages, wiki_page_revisions, wiki_git_commits, rendered_markdown_cache, wiki_assets references when images are linked/uploaded, repository_activity_events, and audit events.

description: Implement wiki history, revision viewing, two-revision compare, and revert controls.
ui_details: History page has heading `History`, a bordered revision list, row checkboxes labelled with commit messages, author avatar/name, committed relative time, short SHA link, and pagination controls for Newer/Older. Compare Revisions button appears above the table when two revisions are selected. Revision detail renders the selected page as of that SHA. Compare view shows file/page diff with added/removed lines and a Revert Changes button for users with edit permission.
behavior: Selecting one revision enables viewing that revision via the SHA link. Selecting two revisions enables Compare Revisions and navigates to a diff page. Revert validates edit permission, creates a new wiki commit that restores older content, records the reverted revision in the commit message, and redirects to the page/history. Pagination preserves page scope and moves through older/newer wiki commits. Users without edit permission can view history and diffs but do not see revert controls.
data_model: Reads wiki_repositories, wiki_pages, wiki_page_revisions, wiki_git_commits, wiki_diff_cache, repository_permissions, and users. Writes wiki_git_commits, wiki_page_revisions, rendered_markdown_cache, wiki_revert_events, repository_activity_events, notifications where enabled, and audit events for revert operations.

description: Implement repository label management for listing, searching, sorting, creating, editing, deleting, and applying labels across issues, pull requests, and discussions.
ui_details: Repository workspace shell with Issues-adjacent Labels page. Header shows `Labels`, search input labelled `Search all labels`, count text such as `86 labels`, Sort button, and permissioned green New label button for write users. Label rows are compact bordered list items with color swatch/pill, label name, description text, open issue/open pull request count links, and permissioned Edit/Delete actions. The sort menu is a radio menu with `Name` and `Total issue count`, plus `Ascending` and `Descending`, with keyboard hints.
behavior: Typing in Search all labels filters labels by name/description and updates the visible count. Clicking a label navigates to an issue/PR query filtered by that label; clicking count links opens open issues or open pull requests with the label qualifier. Sort choices update row order and preserve search state. Write users can open New label/Edit inline form, enter name/description/color, use a random-color button, save changes, and delete after confirmation; deleting removes the label from issues, pull requests, and discussions without deleting the conversations. Triage users can apply/dismiss labels from issue, pull request, and discussion sidebars.
data_model: Reads repositories, repository_permissions, labels, issue_labels, pull_request_labels, discussion_labels, issues, pull_requests, discussions, and organization_default_labels. Writes labels, issue_labels/pull_request_labels/discussion_labels when applied from metadata sidebars, label rename/color/description revisions, issue_timeline_events, pull_request_timeline_events, discussion_events, notifications where label changes are subscribed, and audit events for create/edit/delete.

description: Implement repository milestone management for list, detail, progress, issue prioritization, creation, editing, closing, reopening, and deletion.
ui_details: Repository workspace shell with Milestones page. List view has Open and Closed tabs with counts, Sort button, permissioned New milestone button, and milestone cards/rows showing title link, due date or No due date, last updated timestamp, horizontal progress bar, percent complete, open issue count link, and closed issue count link. Sort menu options include Recently updated, Furthest due date, Closest due date, Least complete, Most complete, Alphabetical, Reverse alphabetical, Most issues, and Fewest issues. Detail page shows Back to Milestones, title, New issue button, state pill, due date, last updated text, percent complete, Open/Closed tabs, selectable issue rows with labels/comments/linked PRs/assignees, and permissioned Edit/Close/Reopen/Delete controls.
behavior: Opening /milestones lists open milestones by default; Closed tab shows completed/closed milestones. Sort changes the list order and is shareable in URL state. Opening a milestone shows progress and issue/PR rows scoped to that milestone; New issue preselects the milestone. Selecting rows updates selected count and enables bulk metadata actions where the viewer has permission. Authorized users can create/edit title, due date, and Markdown description; close/reopen changes milestone state; delete removes the milestone but leaves associated issues and pull requests intact with milestone cleared. Issue prioritization by drag/drop is available when a milestone has 500 or fewer open items.
data_model: Reads repositories, repository_permissions, milestones, issues, pull_requests, labels, assignees, linked pull requests, comments counts, milestone_item_order, and rendered_markdown_cache for descriptions. Writes milestones, issue milestone foreign keys, pull request milestone foreign keys, milestone_item_order, milestone state transitions, issue_timeline_events, pull_request_timeline_events, notifications for metadata changes, rendered Markdown cache, and audit events for create/edit/close/reopen/delete.

description: Runner pool and job provisioning. Allocates ephemeral container-based runners, schedules queued jobs onto them by labels, enforces concurrency limits, reports runner health.
ui_details: Admin UI lists runners (online/offline/busy), labels, last seen, queue depth, concurrency settings. New self-hosted runner setup flow.
behavior: Maintain runner registry via heartbeat; assign jobs whose runs-on labels match; back runner with Docker/Fargate; honor cancel-in-progress; mark run failed on runner heartbeat timeout.
data_model: runners(id, name, labels jsonb, status, last_heartbeat, repository_id|null); job_assignments(run_id, job_name, runner_id, started_at, status)

description: Live job log streaming and persistence. Streams stdout/stderr from runners to the UI in near-real-time via SSE and persists raw logs to S3 for replay and download.
ui_details: Log panel with line numbers, search box, expand/collapse step groups, anchor links per line, "Raw" view, gzip download.
behavior: Runner POSTs log chunks via internal API; API appends to S3 multipart stream; SSE endpoint pushes new lines to subscribed browser clients; on completion, finalize multipart upload and mark log finalized_at.
data_model: job_logs(run_id, job_name, s3_key, bytes_written, finalized_at). S3 key pattern: actions/{run_id}/{job}/log.txt

description: Workflow artifact upload/download and dependency cache. Implements the artifact API (actions/upload-artifact, actions/download-artifact) and the cache API (actions/cache).
page: /<owner>/<repo>/actions/runs/<id> artifacts panel + repo Insights cache table
ui_details: Artifact list with name/size/expiration, download button, delete button (write access). Cache table with key/version/size/last-used and delete.
behavior: Internal REST endpoints under /_apis/pipelines/workflows/{run_id}/artifacts and /_apis/artifactcache/cache; S3-backed; honor retention-days; LRU eviction at 10GB per repository.
data_model: artifacts(id, run_id, name, s3_key, size_bytes, expires_at, created_at); caches(id, repository_id, key, version, s3_key, last_used_at, size_bytes)

description: Workflow check-run integration. Each Actions job creates a Check Run on the head SHA, gates merges via branch protection required-checks, exposes per-step annotations.
page: PR Checks tab + commit status badge on commit pages
ui_details: PR Checks list with conclusion icons, "View details" link, expandable annotations panel, "Re-run job" button, required-check badge.
behavior: On job start: create check_runs row with status=in_progress; on completion: set conclusion (success/failure/cancelled/neutral); ingest annotations from ::error::/::warning:: workflow commands; if check is required-for-merge, block PR merge until success.
data_model: check_runs(id, head_sha, repository_id, name, status, conclusion, started_at, completed_at, output_title, output_summary, annotations_count); check_annotations(check_run_id, path, start_line, end_line, level, message)

description: Workflow permissions and repository/organization secrets. Resolves the permissions: block per job, mints a scoped GITHUB_TOKEN per run, exposes encrypted secrets to runners only when authorized.
ui_details: Secrets table (name, updated, environment, action menu); New secret modal; per-environment secrets; environment protection rules toggle; Workflow permissions radio (read-only / read+write); "Allow GitHub Actions to create and approve PRs" toggle.
behavior: Encrypt values with AEAD (libsodium-equivalent) using per-repo data key (KMS-wrapped); inject into runner env as masked vars; redact in logs; permissions narrows GITHUB_TOKEN scopes; environment protection rules require reviewer approval before secrets are released.
data_model: action_secrets(id, scope_type, scope_id, name, ciphertext, key_id, updated_at); environments(id, repository_id, name, deployment_branch_policy jsonb, reviewers jsonb)

description: Background indexing pipeline. Hooks into git ref updates, issue/PR/comment writes, user/org creation; builds tsvector + pg_trgm indexes for code, issues, PRs, repos, users, commits.
ui_details: Backend pipeline. Status visible only to admins under /admin/search.
behavior: Subscribe to internal change events (repo.push, issue.upsert, etc.). For code: walk new tree, extract text from indexable extensions (<=512KB), tokenize, write code_index. For issues/PRs: maintain search columns updated on write. Reindex per-repo on demand.
data_model: code_index(repository_id, ref, path, content_tsv tsvector, content text, language, size_bytes, sha); issues_search(issue_id, search_tsv tsvector, search_trgm text); commit_index(repository_id, sha, author, message_tsv tsvector, committed_at)

description: File finder widget — keyboard-driven `t` shortcut on a repo to fuzzy-find a file by path within the current ref.
ui_details: Modal-style page with single text input, instant fuzzy match against full path list, keyboard nav (up/down), Enter to open. Empty input shows full list. Highlight matched chars.
behavior: API call GET /repos/:owner/:repo/find?ref=... returns the cached path list; client does sublime-style fuzzy scoring locally for snappy UX; no server-side filtering.
data_model: repository_ref_files(repository_id, ref, paths jsonb, updated_at) cache table; client-side scoring only.

description: Active web sessions panel — list devices, locations, signed-in time; revoke individual sessions or sign-out-everywhere.
ui_details: Table: device (parsed UA), browser, IP-derived location, last active, Revoke button per row, Sign-out-everywhere button, "current" badge on this session.
behavior: Revoke deletes session row; current session marked via cookie session id; sign-out-everywhere deletes all sessions for the user except the current one.
data_model: sessions extended with user_agent text, ip_inet inet, last_active_at timestamptz.

description: Security log — append-only audit trail of security-relevant actions (sign-in, sign-out, OAuth link/unlink, email change, sudo confirmations, session revocations) with CSV/JSON export.
ui_details: Table: timestamp, action, IP, location, user-agent. Filter by action type. Pagination 50/page. Export dropdown -> CSV / JSON downloads filtered view.
behavior: Every security event writes one immutable row; viewable only by the account owner; export streams from server with Content-Disposition: attachment.
data_model: security_events(id, user_id, action, ip_inet, user_agent, metadata jsonb, created_at)

description: Global pull-request dashboard at /pulls — every PR the signed-in user opened, was assigned, was requested as reviewer, or that mentions them, across all repositories.
ui_details: Tab bar (Created / Assigned / Mentioned / Review requests). Filter dropdowns (Open/Closed, repo, label, milestone). Sortable list with PR title, repo path, status icon, comment count, last activity, reviewer avatars. Pagination.
behavior: Single SQL with EXISTS clauses over pull_requests, pr_reviewers, pr_mentions; respects repo visibility (no PRs from private repos the user lacks access to).
data_model: Reads pull_requests, pr_reviewers, pr_mentions from repopulls-*.

description: Global issue dashboard at /issues — every issue the signed-in user opened, was assigned, or was mentioned in.
ui_details: Tab bar (Created / Assigned / Mentioned). Filter dropdowns (Open/Closed, repo, label, milestone, project). Sortable list with title, repo path, status icon, comment count, last activity. Pagination.
behavior: Cross-repo SQL respecting visibility; same shape as global pulls.
data_model: Reads issues, issue_assignees, issue_mentions.

description: Site-wide theme, accessibility, and responsive layout system. Light/dark/system theme with Primer-style color tokens, WCAG AA contrast, mobile breakpoints down to 360px, reduced-motion respect.
ui_details: /settings/appearance: theme toggle (light / dark / system / dark dimmed / dark high contrast), font size, "Show preview" panel. Persists via cookie + user_settings. Respects prefers-color-scheme for unauth visitors.
behavior: CSS variables on <html> driven by data-color-mode + data-light-theme/data-dark-theme. Tailwind config maps tokens. All components use tokens (no hardcoded colors). Breakpoint utilities sm/md/lg/xl/2xl.
data_model: user_settings.theme, .font_size; cookie color_mode for unauth visitors.

description: Webhooks event catalog with HMAC signing. Repo and org owners register webhook endpoints; opengithub POSTs JSON payloads on events with X-Hub-Signature-256 (HMAC-SHA256 over the raw body).
ui_details: Hooks table (Active toggle, payload URL, content type, last-delivery icon). Add-webhook form (URL, content type, secret, events checklist, "Just the push event" / "Send me everything" / "Let me select"). Recent deliveries panel with redelivery button + payload viewer.
behavior: Supported events: push, pull_request, pull_request_review, issues, issue_comment, release, workflow_run, check_run, ping. Outbound POST with X-GitHub-Event, X-GitHub-Delivery (uuid), X-Hub-Signature-256. Background worker, 3 retries with exponential backoff.
data_model: webhooks(id, scope_type, scope_id, url, content_type, secret_ciphertext, events jsonb, active, ssl_verify, created_at); webhook_deliveries(id, webhook_id, event, request_headers jsonb, request_body, response_status, response_headers jsonb, response_body, duration_ms, redelivery_of uuid|null, delivered_at)

description: REST API rate limiting and API versioning. Returns X-RateLimit-Limit/Remaining/Reset/Used/Resource on every API response. Honors X-GitHub-Api-Version request header (e.g. 2022-11-28).
ui_details: Backend; visible in response headers. /rate_limit endpoint exposes current bucket state.
behavior: Per-token sliding-window limiter (per-IP for anon). Tiers: 5000/hr authenticated, 60/hr anon, 30/min for search. 403 on overage with body { error: { code: "rate_limited", message: ... } }. Versioning: server holds latest version; older clients pin via header.
data_model: rate_limit_buckets(token_id|ip, resource, window_start, request_count) — Redis-equivalent or Postgres unlogged table.

description: Social loop primitives: star, fork, watch (subscribe), and follow (user-to-user). Drives the home activity feed, repo discoverability, and notification fan-out.
ui_details: Star button with count and hover popover "Add to list" submenu (named star lists). Fork modal: destination owner, default name with -fork suffix, "Copy main branch only" toggle. Watch dropdown (Participating / All / Releases only / Custom / Ignore). Follow button on profiles + suggested-people sidebar. /stars page with list filter, sort, search.
behavior: Stars: insert into stars(user_id, repo_id, list_id|null). Forks: server-side git clone within S3-backed git storage (shared object dedup), new repository row owned by destination with parent_repository_id set, refs copied. Watch: subscriptions(user_id, repo_id, mode) feeds notification routing. Follow: follows(follower_id, followee_id) feeds the home feed query.
data_model: stars(user_id, repository_id, list_id nullable, created_at); star_lists(id, user_id, name, description, is_private); subscriptions(user_id, repository_id, mode enum, ignored_at nullable); follows(follower_id, followee_id, created_at). repositories extended with parent_repository_id, source_repository_id, forks_count, stargazers_count.

description: Gists — single-file or multi-file snippets at /gist/<id> with their own git history, public/secret visibility, fork/star, embeds, and revisions.
ui_details: New-gist form with file tabs (+ add file), per-file language detection, Public vs Secret toggle, description input. View page with embed snippet (<script src=...>), Star/Fork buttons (reuses social-001), comments section. Profile sidebar gets a Public gists widget.
behavior: Each gist is a real git repo backed by the same S3-backed storage; `git clone https://opengithub.namuh.co/gist/<id>.git` works. Embed endpoint returns a self-contained <div> + token CSS to render in third-party pages. Secret gists are unlisted (URL-only access, not in search) but not auth-gated.
data_model: gists(id, owner_id, description, is_public, created_at, updated_at); gist_files(gist_id, filename, language, size_bytes, content_sha); gist_comments mirrors issue_comments shape.

description: AI surface — repository auto-summary card, AI-generated PR summary + suggested reviewers, AI changelog from commit history. Uses the same Codex/OpenAI provider backing the build loop.
page: repo header (AI summary card), /<owner>/<repo>/pull/<n> (AI summary tab), /<owner>/<repo>/releases/<tag> (Generate changelog with AI button)
ui_details: Repo summary card under repo header with skeleton loader + Regenerate action, fed by README + top-level files + recent commit messages. PR AI tab: TL;DR, files-of-interest list with one-line risk note each, suggested reviewers (CODEOWNERS + recent committers heuristic), inline-comment seed visible only to PR author. AI changelog modal in releases (Compare from <prev tag>) with grouped Added/Changed/Fixed/Deprecated bullets, Edit before publish.
behavior: Inference behind /api/ai/* endpoints. Provider: OpenAI REST (api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions) using OPENAI_API_KEY from env. Models: gpt-4o-mini for cheap calls (repo summary, hover blurbs), gpt-4o for PR summaries and changelog generation. Per-repo opt-in setting; private-repo content never leaves the cluster unless toggled. Server-side cache keyed on (content_hash, prompt_version, model). Per-user rate-limited via api-003 buckets. For PR summary: send diff (truncated to ~30k tokens), file tree, commit messages. For changelog: send commit log between two refs.
data_model: ai_outputs(id, kind enum [repo_summary, pr_summary, changelog, ...], scope_type, scope_id, content_hash, prompt_version, model, output text, generated_at, regenerated_count); user_settings.ai_features_enabled; repository_settings.ai_features_enabled.
